evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern(String seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) bundles tuning params into ExploreOptions, but the sibling method exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig, double minLogDice, int topCollocates, int minShared) takes the same logical parameters as 3 flat args instead

QueryExecutor interface mixes result-type families and threshold responsibility: findCollocations/executeSurfacePattern/executeDependencyPattern all accept minLogDice and maxResults and return WordSketchResult, while executeQuery/executeBcqlQuery drop minLogDice entirely and return ConcordanceResult/CollocateResult — the split is justified by purpose but the naming (findCollocations vs executeQuery vs executeBcqlQuery) obscures which methods threshold results internally

HttpApiUtils methods are fully consistent: sendJsonResponse(exchange, data), sendError(exchange, code, message), sendOptionsResponse(exchange, allowedMethods), sendBinaryResponse(exchange, contentType, bytes) — uniform exchange-first convention throughout

All ExplorationHandlers handler methods follow the same shape: parse query string → requireParam null-check → resolveRelationConfig → parseExploreParams → call explorer → build response Map → sendJsonResponse; error paths are uniformly handled via sendError

compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seeds, double minLogDice, int topCollocates) uses flat params — same flat-param style as exploreMultiSeed, but ExploreOptions bundles an overlapping set (topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared) for exploreByPattern

strengths: HttpApiUtils is a model of API consistency: all response-sending methods take HttpExchange as their first argument with predictable subsequent parameters, and the class is exclusively static utilities with no state

QueryExecutor is thoroughly documented with Javadoc that explicitly distinguishes the four execution styles (CQL vs BCQL, concordance vs collocate), reducing the cognitive cost of the naming asymmetry

All handler methods in ExplorationHandlers share a uniform control-flow skeleton (requireParam → resolveRelationConfig → parseExploreParams → service call → sendJsonResponse), making the error-path shape predictable across endpoints
ExploreOptions exists as a parameter-object for exploreByPattern, demonstrating that the team recognized the value of bundling related tuning knobs
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: The core issue is intra-class inconsistency within SemanticFieldExplorer: the ExploreOptions parameter-object pattern exists and is used for one public method but not extended to sibling methods that accept overlapping parameters as flat args.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The HTTP-layer API (HttpApiUtils, handler methods) is consistently shaped and the QueryExecutor interface is well-annotated, which pushes quality above 60. However, the public SemanticFieldExplorer API is split: exploreByPattern uses ExploreOptions while exploreMultiSeed and compareCollocateProfiles take the same logical parameters as flat args, creating inconsistency at the most-used service-layer boundary. This is not a deeply structural problem — it is fixable with an overload — but a developer navigating between these methods will notice the mismatch and cannot apply the same call pattern. Against global anchors, this is solidly 'significant drag that is not ubiquitous,' placing the score around 70.

issues.summary: exploreMultiSeed takes flat minLogDice/topCollocates/minShared args while sibling exploreByPattern uses ExploreOptions for the same parameters
evidence: exploreByPattern(String seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions opts) bundles topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared into ExploreOptions
exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig relationConfig, double minLogDice, int topCollocates, int minShared) spreads the same logical parameters as positional arguments
compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seeds, double minLogDice, int topCollocates) also uses flat params, reinforcing the split
issues.suggestion: Add an overload exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) that delegates to the current flat-param implementation, and do the same for compareCollocateProfiles; update ExplorationHandlers to call the options-taking overloads so the service layer sees a uniform shape

root_causes: ExploreOptions was introduced to replace flat params for the single-seed path but the multi-seed path was added later (or in a separate iteration) without applying the same pattern, leaving the API surface split across two conventions
